# Opportunities with AD Aerospace {#w3a}

Our friends at AD Aerospace (see figure \@ref(fig:adaero-fig)) in Stockport are looking for a 3D Artist (see section \@ref(threed)) and a Frontend Website Developer (see section \@ref(frontend)) to work as summer interns.

```{r adaero-fig, echo = FALSE, fig.align = "center", out.width = "100%", fig.cap = "(ref:captionadaero)"}
knitr::include_graphics("images/adaero.png")
```

(ref:captionadaero) AD Aerospace is part of the Mythra Group of companies, pioneers and market leaders in state-of-the-art CCTV solutions. Screenshot of company website [ad-aero.com](https://ad-aero.com/) 

## 3D Artist – Aerospace Vision Systems {#threed}

AD Aerospace are looking for a student to work creating 3D content for marketing materials for a new 4K camera product for aerospace vision use cases, such as Cargo Handling and Ground Manoeuvring.

You should have a keen interest in 3D Rendering with good problem solving skills and the ability to think creatively and laterally. Whilst you will develop your skills through your course of delivering projects it is essential that you have good basic skills in:

* Proficient in 3D modelling software such as Maya, 3ds Max, or Blender
* Ability to create high-quality 3D models, textures, and animations for use in video, or other media
* Strong understanding of lighting, shading, and rendering techniques
* Experience with rigging and animation of characters and objects
* Knowledge of industry-standard workflows and pipelines
* Ability to work collaboratively with other artists, designers, and programmers
* Strong attention to detail and ability to meet project deadlines.  
* Experience with VR/AR development is a plus. 
* A portfolio showcasing previous 3D modelling and animation work is required.

You must be able to demonstrate knowledge and understanding of these principles, for example, by presenting marks for degree modules completed. It is expected that your skillset will be beyond the course curriculum having been self developed through your desire to learn.

We are looking for a flexible, hardworking and motivated individual with an inquiring mind, and a proactive approach. Being part of a team who are looking at the next thing in surveillance systems, you will enjoy taking part in discussions exploring both practical and theoretical solutions to ideas and be excited to take them to the next step of the development process.

Interested candidates should submit a CV with links to portfolio work to our retained consultant via email - `talent@w3associates.co.uk`

## Frontend Website Developer – Surveillance Systems {#frontend}

AD are also seeking a student to continue the development software solutions in exciting research projects encompassing aircraft video surveillance. One such project is developing a microsite for a new product.

You should have a keen interest in frontend website development with good problem solving skills and the ability to think creatively and laterally.
Whilst you will develop your skills through the course of delivering projects, it is important that you have good basic skills in:

* Excellent HTML, CSS, JavaScript skills
* Coding valid HTML5 and CSS3 / 4 mark-up
* Developing cross-browser and cross-platform compatible solutions
* Experience with SASS / BeM Coding Standards
* Experience working with version control systems such as GIT
* Experience with Javascript frameworks (AngularJS, React)

You must be able to demonstrate knowledge and understanding of these principles, for example, by presenting marks for degree modules completed. It is expected that your skillset will be beyond the course curriculum having been self developed through your desire to learn.

We are looking for a flexible, hardworking and motivated individual with an inquiring mind, and a proactive approach. Being part of a team who are looking at the next thing in surveillance systems, you will enjoy taking part in discussions exploring both practical and theoretical solutions to ideas and be excited to take them to the next step of the development process.

Interested candidates should submit a CV with links to portfolio work to our retained consultant via email - `talent@w3associates.co.uk`
